outcome: Paley I gives H(168), not H(668). Paley II requires q≡1(mod4), but 167≡3(mod4). Both provably inapplicable.
Main approach. Legendre baseline achieves PSD=672 (gap=4). SA optimization over ~500M evaluations reaches L2~400K, Linf~120. Solution not found but approach has lowest known gap.
668 cannot be factored into two valid Hadamard orders. Provably inapplicable.
Williamson restriction reduces search space to 2^336 but SA still plateaus. No Williamson solution found for n=167. Possible that Williamson matrices don't exist for this order.
Four Legendre PAF total = -4 at all non-zero shifts (not 0). The gap of 4 is fundamental. Valid DC tuples (row sum decompositions) cataloged: 10 solutions.
ROOT OBSTRUCTION: 4 does not divide |Z_167*|=166=2×83. No useful 4-class cyclotomic decomposition exists. The sparse group structure (83 prime) is likely why H(668) is harder than neighboring orders.

PSD must equal 668 at all 167 frequencies. Energy conservation: sum|X(k)|^2 = p^2 per sequence. Reducing PSD at one frequency necessarily increases it at others. This spectral rigidity is the core optimization barrier.
Theoretical potential via QAOA (Suksmono 2025), but current quantum hardware lacks scale for 668 variables. Not computationally feasible with current technology.
Eliahou (2025) achieved 64-modular result (HH^T ≡ 668I mod 64). Our Legendre baseline achieves mod-4 equivalence. Gap from modular to exact remains the key challenge.
Conference matrices of order 168 exist (Paley). But conference matrix border construction doesn't directly yield H(668). Alternative structural approach with no clear advantage.
T-sequences require factorization 167 = t × s, but 167 is prime. Only t=1 (standard Williamson) or t=167 (unknown T-matrices). No viable decomposition.
Spence SDS parameters 4-{334;167,167,167,168;334} are valid but search space is 2x larger than GS approach in Z_167. Not computationally pursued beyond parameter analysis.
